---
title: "Veteran Lung Cancer Survival Analysis"
format: html
---

This project applies survival analysis to a Veterans Administration randomised
controlled trial comparing standard vs experimental chemotherapy in patients
with advanced, inoperable lung cancer. It uses Kaplan–Meier estimation, Cox
proportional hazards modelling, spline transformations, interaction testing,
and the Aalen additive model — all implemented in R with `survival`,
`survminer`, and `ggplot2`, rendered via Quarto.

The project is organised into three parts:

---

## Overview

A concise summary of the dataset, key survival estimates, and the main model
findings — four plots that tell the full story at a glance.

👉 [Open Overview »](notebooks/veteran-lung-cancer-overview.html)

---

## Full Report

A complete step-by-step analysis: exploratory survival analysis, model
building, functional form checking, spline transformations, interaction
testing, diagnostics, and final model interpretation.

👉 [Open Full Report »](notebooks/veteran-lung-cancer-coxph-report.html)

---

## Teaching & Learning Reference

The same analysis with collapsible callout sections explaining the statistical
reasoning behind each step — theory, assumptions, code decisions, and
diagnostics. A reusable reference for future survival analyses.

👉 [Open Teaching Notebook »](notebooks/veteran-lung-cancer-coxph.html)
